records.ety: [A frequentative fr. OE. manken to main, AS. mancian, in bemancian to mutilate, fr. L. mancus maimed; perh. akin to G. mangeln to be wanting.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mangled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mangling (?).]

defs: To cut or bruise with repeated blows or strokes, making a ragged or torn wound, or covering with wounds; to tear in cutting; to cut in a bungling manner; to lacerate; to mutilate.
quotes: Mangled with ghastly wounds through plate and mail.

defs: To mutilate or injure, in making, doing, or performing; as, to mangle a piece of music or a recitation.
quotes: To mangle a play or a novel.

records.ety: [D. mangel, fr. OE. mangonel a machine for throwing stones, LL. manganum, Gr. <?/ a machine for defending fortifications, axis of a pulley. Cf. Mangonel.]

defs: A machine for smoothing linen or cotton cloth, as sheets, tablecloths, napkins, and clothing, by roller pressure, often with heated rollers.
To smooth with a mangle, as damp linen or cloth.
